rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL GIEDGOWD VS. KEVIN MCGUINNESS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Michael Giedgowd'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 17, 2012, Line 1, Plaintiff Michael Giedgowd's Motion For Attorney's Fees And Costs. Denied. Despite the court's order of May 2 requiring the plaintiff to provide courtesy copies of the moving and any reply papers by May 3, no such papers have been received. Plaintiff's non-compliance with Local Rule 2.6B requires denial of his motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELICA M. GONZALEZ VS. ALFONSO PICAZO, AS TRUSTEES OF THE ARTHUR AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Alfonso Picazo And Martha Picazo'S Motion To Quash Subpoena For Tax Records Or In The Alternative For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 17, 2012, Line 25, Plaintiffs Alfonso Picazo And Martha Picazo'S Motion To Quash Subpoena For Tax Records Or In The Alternative For A Protective Order. The motion to quash is denied and the alternative request for a protective order is granted in part and denied in part. The request that responsive documents be provided to a CPA is denied and the request that the scope of the subpoena be limited and information not relating to the property at issue in this suit be redacted is granted. Inter-Americana shall immediately produce responsive documents to Plaintiff. The scope of the subpoena shall be limited to the period from 2005 until 2010. Inter-Americana shall redact any information not relating to the properties at issue in this litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: FRED G. MEIS ESQ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Petition For Revocation Of Assignment For Discharge Of Assignee
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 17, 2012, Line 11, PETITIONER FRED MEIS, KERRIN MEIS (Amended) Petition For Revocation Of Assignment For Discharge Of Assignee. Denied. Petitioners are not entitled to equitable relief to revoke the irrevocable Assignment for the Benefit Creditors. Since the filing of the petition, Respondent has provided Petitioners with an accounting and Respondent offered to turn over to the client files still in its possession. The court encourages the parties to meet and confer in an effort to reach an agreement to conclude all of the Respondent's rights and duties under the Assignment.
